steps.name: Code is merged into main/trunk
steps.description: All developers can now see, use, and build on this code. You cannot build the release candidate before this step is completed.

steps.name: Build snapshot version
steps.description: Snapshot of code in time is packaged into an identifiable version for testing purposes and not suitable for production.

steps.name: Deploy snapshot version to production-like test environment (INT)
steps.description: Deploy to protected pre-production environment with production-like hardware, interfaces and data

steps.name: Continuous snapshot end to end testing
steps.description: These tests exercise business flows of the whole system, across applications, including 3rd party systems. This may be manual or automated.

steps.name: Build release candidate
steps.description: Code is built and packaged into a release candidate.  This step can't be done before code is merged into main/trunk.

steps.name: Deploy to production-like test environment (SYS)
steps.description: Deploy to protected pre-production environment with production-like hardware, interfaces and data.

steps.name: End to end testing
steps.description: These tests exercise business flows of the whole system, across application, including 3rd party systems. This may be manual or automated.

steps.name: User acceptance testing
steps.description: End user representatives validate the functionality works in the way the want it to work.

steps.name: Go/No-Go decision
steps.description: Stakeholders come together to assess information and decide on deployment.

beforeSteps.name: Unit testing
beforeSteps.description: Tests that don't require build and deploy, written by developers, are run against implementation.

afterSteps.name: Testing in production
afterSteps.description: Testing in production after production deploy. Done due to uncertainties between different environments

parkingLot.name: Automated performance testing
parkingLot.description: Assess the scale of use for concurrent users, data volumes and anything with system constraints.

parkingLot.name: Usability testing
parkingLot.description: Experts observe real users on the system to assess how they use features and if the expected benefits can be acquired.

parkingLot.name: 3rd party integration testing
parkingLot.description: Verify the system talks to 3rd party systems correctly. The 3rd party provides a test version of their system.

parkingLot.name: Automated functional testing
parkingLot.description: These tests execute a function of the system, checking both happy paths and error conditions. They use an API to access the functionality.

parkingLot.name: Contract testing
parkingLot.description: Verify the software will integrate correctly with 3rd party service by ensuring it fulfills the contract required by that service. The contract test should be supplied by the 3rd party and run against our software.

parkingLot.name: Security testing
parkingLot.description: Skilled tester explore the system for security issues.

parkingLot.name: Static code analysis
parkingLot.description: Run a tool on the source code producing information on complexity and coverage, and technical risks (security, technical debt). Compare statistics to expected baseline.

parkingLot.name: Deployment health check
parkingLot.description: Verify software has been deployed correctly with limited checks. Usually checks things like all services are registered and available, needed ports are open, and logs are being produced.

parkingLot.name: Decide package version number
parkingLot.description: When package is created, it needs a unique identifier. Internal and external identifier may be separate. Package version number may be automatically generated or manually assigned.

parkingLot.name: AI Code review
parkingLot.description: Agent creates a code review for original author to assess for actions.

parkingLot.name: Code review
parkingLot.description: Second pair of eyes looks at the code.

parkingLot.name: Automated user interface testing
parkingLot.description: Tests exercise a wide variety of functionality, using the UI in the same way as actual users.

parkingLot.name: Automated smoke test
parkingLot.description: Some very basic feature is verified. Nothing more is tested.

parkingLot.name: Deploy on build node
parkingLot.description: The whole system is deployed on a single machine. 3rd party services are replaced by test doubles or unavailable. Databased, load balancers and queues may be replaced with in-memory alternatives.

parkingLot.name: Component testing
parkingLot.description: Test only one component of the system. Replace the other systems with test doubles.

parkingLot.name: Create release notes
parkingLot.description: With release candidate we publish information about what is new in the release. The release notes may be assembled by hand or created automatically.
